title: Listen and Choose a Response — Social Interactions

questions.stem: I just found out I didn't get the internship I applied for.

options.A: Don't worry, there will be other opportunities.
options.B: When did you submit your application?
options.C: I got an internship last summer too.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing disappointment about bad news, so an empathetic and reassuring response is most appropriate.

questions.stem: These boxes are really heavy. Could you give me a hand?

options.A: Sure, where do you want them?
options.C: The store is having a sale on boxes.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for physical help, so a willing offer to assist is the natural response.

questions.stem: Guess what? I got accepted into the graduate program!
questions.explanation: The speaker is sharing exciting personal news, so an enthusiastic and congratulatory response is expected.

questions.stem: I'm not sure I understand how to use this software. It's so complicated.

options.A: I felt the same way when I first started.
options.B: Software updates are released every month.
options.C: Have you tried reading the manual from page one?

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing confusion and implicitly asking for help, so offering a concrete starting point for learning is most helpful.

questions.stem: The Wi-Fi in the dorm has been really slow for the past three days.

options.A: You should buy a new laptop.
options.B: That's frustrating. Have you reported it to IT services?
options.C: I don't use Wi-Fi very often.

questions.explanation: The speaker is complaining about a problem, so acknowledging their frustration and suggesting a constructive next step is appropriate.

questions.stem: A few of us are going to the concert on Saturday. Want to come?

options.A: I don't really like loud music.
options.B: Sounds fun! What time should I meet you?

questions.explanation: The speaker is extending a social invitation, so an enthusiastic acceptance with a practical follow-up question is the natural response.

questions.stem: So, the meeting is at 3 PM in Room 302, not Room 203, right?

options.B: I have another meeting at 4 PM.

questions.explanation: The speaker is seeking confirmation of specific details, so directly confirming or correcting the information is appropriate.

questions.stem: I really appreciate you covering my shift yesterday. You saved me!

options.A: It was no problem at all.
options.B: I worked three shifts last week.
options.C: You should plan your schedule better.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing strong gratitude, so a humble and gracious acceptance is the polite response.

audioSegments.text: I just found out I didn't get the internship I applied for.
These boxes are really heavy. Could you give me a hand?
Guess what? I got accepted into the graduate program!
I'm not sure I understand how to use this software. It's so complicated.
The Wi-Fi in the dorm has been really slow for the past three days.
A few of us are going to the concert on Saturday. Want to come?
So, the meeting is at 3 PM in Room 302, not Room 203, right?
I really appreciate you covering my shift yesterday. You saved me!
